Canton Ticino has implemented social reintegration programs and invested in the training of penitentiary staff. These efforts have led to an improvement in the living conditions of inmates and a reduction in the recidivism rate. However, the comparison with the Italian situation highlights the need for further interventions to guarantee rights and dignity to all inmates.

## Useful tools to protect your net income
To reduce FX leakage, compare [CHF-EUR exchange options](nav:exchange) and [banks for cross-border workers](nav:banks). ### What to do

Faced with this critical situation, it is essential that Italian authorities take urgent measures to improve prison conditions and guarantee the fundamental rights of inmates. Here are some concrete steps that can be taken:

1. Increase the number of available places: It is necessary to build new prison structures or expand existing ones to reduce overcrowding.
2. **Hire more staff**: The shortage of prison staff must be addressed by hiring new agents and providing adequate training.
3. Improve living conditions: It is essential to ensure dignified living conditions for inmates, with adequate spaces and health and psychological services.
4. Promote social reintegration: Social reintegration programs must be implemented to help inmates reintegrate into society once released.

### Useful tools

For those who wish to delve deeper into the situation of Italian prisons and possible solutions, the following tools may be useful:

- [Salary calculator](nav:calculator): to understand the economic implications of prison work.
- [Guide to social reintegration](nav:residency): for information on how to access social reintegration programs.
- [Legal assistance](nav:health): for those in need of legal support in penitentiary matters.

### Conclusion

Cecilia Strada's denunciation to the European Parliament has highlighted a critical situation in Italian prisons, particularly in the prison of Busto Arsizio. It is fundamental that Italian authorities take urgent measures to improve prison conditions and guarantee the fundamental rights of inmates. Only in this way can a further deterioration of the situation be avoided and the social reintegration of inmates promoted.

*Source: [varesenews.it](https://www.varesenews.it/2026/04/carcere-di-busto-la-denuncia-di-cecilia-strada-al-parlamento-europeo-sovraffollamento-oltre-il-200-e-un-inferno/2561764/)*

item.title: Luigi Bignami at Insubria: the art of science communication
item.description: Geologist and science journalist Luigi Bignami met with students at the University of Insubria to discuss science communication.
item.content:encoded:
## In brief
- Luigi Bignami at Insubria to talk about scientific communication
- Scientific dissemination requires rigor and accessibility
- Bignami: 'Simplifying is not trivializing'

## Key facts
- **What**: Lecture by Luigi Bignami on scientific communication
- When: Wednesday, April 29, 2026
- Where: Università degli Studi dell'Insubria, Varese
- Who: Luigi Bignami, geologist and science journalist
- Fee: Not specified

The renowned science communicator Luigi Bignami met with students at Università degli Studi dell'Insubria to explain how to translate complexity to the general public without trivializing it. The lecture, organized as part of the Science Communication course directed by professor Federico Pasquarè Mariotto, offered students a clear picture of the science communicator's profession. Bignami emphasized that science communication is a job that requires rigor, constant study, and the ability to be understood without reducing complexity to something banal.

### The professional turning point
Bignami's professional turning point came after the Valtellina flood in 1987. During that event, Bignami realized the gap that existed between the technical knowledge of scientists and the public's perception. 'I knew what was happening, but the people didn't. Until the last moment, they hoped that nothing serious would ever happen,' he recounted. That episode was the spark that ignited in Bignami the desire to learn how to translate geological data into stories accessible to everyone.

### Simplifying without trivializing
During the lecture, Bignami insisted on the need to build a relationship of trust with the reader and with the editorial staff. 'People are intelligent, but they need someone capable of explaining important information to them in a language they can understand. Only in this way can a communicator earn their trust,' he stated. To achieve this result, preparation is the only effective tool. Bignami illustrated his method, based on daily study, cross-analysis of sources, and the ability to write texts that capture attention from the first lines.

### The audiovisual language
Part of the meeting was dedicated to the audiovisual language, with a focus on documentaries. Bignami explained how multimedia such as television allow enriching the scientific message, succeeding in transmitting in an immediate and engaging way even complex concepts, which would require rivers of words to be described.

### The challenges of the sector
The intervention also touched on the working dynamics of the sector. Bignami recounted the difficulties encountered in proposing scientific topics to directors and editors-in-chief, emphasizing the importance of determination. 'You have to explain to them why a topic is important, insist, never give up,' he said. After all, the role of the science communicator is becoming increasingly important. 'Today, it is very difficult to distinguish scientific reality from personal opinions. Our role is to make people fall in love with science,' Bignami concluded. ### Implications for Scientific Communication
Luigi Bignami's lecture at the University of Insubria highlighted the importance of effective and accessible scientific communication. For journalists and communicators, this means adopting an approach that balances rigor and simplicity. Bignami emphasized that the ability to translate complex concepts into understandable language is fundamental to gaining the public's trust.

### The Importance of Training
Another crucial aspect that emerged during the meeting is the importance of continuous training. Bignami explained that his method is based on daily study and cross-analysis of sources. This approach not only improves the quality of communication but also helps maintain the reader's attention. For young communicators, this means investing time and resources in their own training to always be up-to-date and prepared.

### The Role of the Media
Bignami also spoke about the role of the media in scientific dissemination. He emphasized that the media have the responsibility to transmit accurate and understandable information. This is particularly important in an era where misinformation is widespread. The media must be a bridge between the scientific world and the public, ensuring that information is transmitted clearly and precisely.

### Challenges and Opportunities
The lecture also addressed the challenges and opportunities in the field of scientific communication. Bignami spoke about the difficulties in proposing scientific topics to editors and news editors, emphasizing the importance of determination. He also highlighted the importance of insisting and not giving up, even in the face of obstacles. For young communicators, this means being ready to face challenges and always seek new opportunities to spread scientific knowledge.

### Conclusion
In conclusion, Luigi Bignami's lecture at the University of Insubria offered students a comprehensive overview of the profession of a scientific communicator. He emphasized the importance of effective communication, continuous training, and the crucial role of the media. For young communicators, this means being ready to face the challenges of the sector with determination and passion.

## Recommended tools
For an updated estimate, use the [net salary calculator](nav:calculator) and the [CHF-EUR exchange comparator](nav:exchange). ### How to Become a Good Science Communicator
To become a good science communicator, it's essential to follow some concrete steps. First, it's important to invest in your education. This means constantly studying, staying updated on the latest scientific discoveries, and developing the ability to translate complex concepts into understandable language. Bignami emphasized the importance of a methodical approach based on daily study and cross-referencing sources.

### How to Face the Challenges of the Sector
Facing the challenges of scientific communication requires determination and resilience. Bignami advised being persistent in proposing scientific topics to editors and head editors. It's important to clearly explain why a topic is relevant and insist until its value is recognized. Additionally, it's crucial to build a trusting relationship with the public, providing accurate and understandable information.

### Useful Tools for Scientific Communication
There are several tools that can help scientific communicators improve their effectiveness. For example, the use of images and videos can enrich the scientific message, making it more engaging and immediate. Additionally, cross-referencing sources can help ensure the accuracy of information. Bignami emphasized the importance of using clear and precise language, avoiding oversimplifications.

### Conclusion and CTA
In conclusion, Luigi Bignami's lesson at the University of Insubria offered valuable advice on becoming a good science communicator. To delve deeper, visit our [calculator](nav:calculator) to discover how to apply these techniques in your career. Additionally, consult our guides on [scientific communication](nav:health) for more tips and useful tools.

*Source: [varesenews.it](https://www.varesenews.it/2026/04/luigi-bignami-allinsubria-racconta-larte-di-comunicare-la-scienza-tra-rigore-e-accessibilita/2561473/)*

item.title: Svicc evicts trainers, closes Varese stables
item.description: Svicc evicts trainers from Varese stables within 60 days. Demolition and renovation project underway.
item.content:encoded:
## In brief
- Svicc evicts trainers within 60 days
- Contracts valid until 2033 according to trainers
- Demolition and renovation project

## Key facts
- **What**: Eviction of stable trainers
- When: Communication of April 16, 2026
- Where: Stables on via Galdino, Varese
- Who: Società Varesina Incremento Corse Cavalli (Svicc)
- Amount: Between 150 and 200 thoroughbreds in the stables

Svicc has notified the trainers of the stables on via Galdino in Varese of the termination of the lease contracts and the obligation to leave the boxes within 60 days. The communication of April 16 came after years of tension between the company and the trainers. Svicc has contested alleged contractual breaches, including the failure to deliver insurance policies and documentation on the horses.

The company also communicated the refusal to renew the contracts expiring in 2027, justifying it with a demolition and renovation project of the entire via Galdino complex. According to Svicc, this plan makes the presence of the trainers in the stables incompatible.

### Trainers' response
The trainers, through Grizzetti Galoppo, have fully contested Svicc's reconstruction. According to the defense, the contract would still be valid and already renewed until 2033. The alleged violations have been defined as 'generic and unproven', while the cessation of the hippodrome's concession would not justify the termination of the relationship.

The defense has invited the company to refrain from initiatives that could compromise the activity. The issue of the stables and the trainers' presence directly intersects with the future use of the hippodrome and the restart of sports activities.

### The role of the Municipality
The Municipality of Varese, owner of the Bettole hippodrome, has entrusted the new management of the facility to Varese Turf & Sport after the end of the concession to Svicc. The municipal administration will necessarily have to take a position on the issue of the stables and the trainers' presence.

The new management has started maintenance and relaunch works of the facility, with the aim of reopening by June 10. Interventions that concern stands, tracks and services, to return to the city a historic sports space. However, without a solution to the stables' issue, the relaunch risks starting with a heavy unknown because without the horses. ### Implications for Varesina Horse Racing
The conflict between Svicc and the trainers of the stables on via Galdino has significant implications for Varesina horse racing. Without the facilities of the stables, the training activity risks coming to a halt, with direct consequences on the well-being of the horses and the economic activities linked to the racecourse.

Over the past fifteen years, the stables on via Galdino have been the scene of a recurring clash, often conducted with controversial methods. The cutting off of utilities, water, light, and gas, has been repeatedly evoked as a tool of pressure by the owners to prompt the payment of overdue box fees. The trainers have long denounced the conditions of a structure now deemed dilapidated and in need of extraordinary maintenance interventions.

### Future Scenarios
If Svicc were to prevail in the legal dispute, the project to demolish and restructure the entire via Galdino complex could lead to an overall redevelopment of the area. However, this scenario would entail the temporary suspension of training activities and could have a negative impact on the local horse racing sector.

On the other hand, if the trainers manage to defend the validity of their contracts, the situation could stabilize, allowing the continuation of training activities and the reopening of the racecourse. However, the issue of the condition of the stables and the need for extraordinary maintenance interventions would remain open.

### The Role of the Municipality and the New Management
The Municipality of Varese, owner of the Bettole racecourse, has entrusted the new management of the facility to Varese Turf & Sport. The new management has started maintenance and revitalization work on the facility, with the aim of reopening by June 10. However, the issue of the stables and the presence of the trainers remains a crucial point for the future of the racecourse.

The municipal administration will necessarily have to take a position on the matter, balancing the needs of redeveloping the area with the necessity of ensuring the continuity of training activities and the reopening of the racecourse.

## Recommended tools
For an updated estimate, use the [net salary calculator](nav:calculator) and the [CHF-EUR exchange comparator](nav:exchange). ### What trainers should do
Trainers at the stables on Galdino Street in Varese are facing a complex and delicate situation. First and foremost, it is crucial to consult a lawyer specializing in real estate law and lease agreements to assess their legal options and defend the validity of their lease agreements.

Additionally, trainers should gather all documentation related to lease agreements, insurance policies, and horse records to present as evidence in support of their position. It is also important to maintain constant communication with Svicc and the Municipality of Varese to seek a negotiated solution that can ensure the continuity of training activities.

### Next steps
Trainers should also consider the possibility of joining an association or committee to collectively represent their interests and increase their negotiating power. Moreover, it is important to closely monitor the maintenance and redevelopment work at the racetrack to ensure that the needs of the stables and trainers are taken into account.

Finally, trainers should consider the possibility of finding temporary alternatives for horse training in case of forced eviction. This could include searching for other facilities or collaborating with other racetracks or training centers in the area.

### Useful tools
For trainers facing this complex situation, tools such as the [rental cost calculator](nav:calculator) and the [lease agreement comparator](nav:permit-compare) can be useful. These tools can help evaluate the available options and make informed decisions.

Additionally, it is important to consult a specialized lawyer to obtain personalized advice and defend their rights. Do not hesitate to contact a professional to ensure the best possible defense.

*Source: [varesenews.it](https://www.varesenews.it/2026/05/ippodromo-scontro-sulle-scuderie-la-svicc-sfratta-gli-allenatori/2563258/)*

item.title: Free Museum Entry in Swimwear at Beyeler Foundation
item.description: The Beyeler Foundation in Riehen offers free entry in swimwear for a day dedicated to Paul Cézanne and his paintings of bathers.
item.content:encoded:
## In brief
- Free museum entry in swimwear 
- Initiative inspired by the Paul Cézanne exhibition 
- High attendance expected in the coming weekends 

## Key facts
- **What**: Swimmers' Day with free entry 
- When: May 1, 2026 
- Where: Fondation Beyeler, Riehen (BS) 
- Who: Fondation Beyeler 
- Cost: Free entry 

The Fondation Beyeler in Riehen, located in the canton of Basel-Stadt, has launched an initiative as unusual as it is fascinating. On May 1, 2026, the museum will open its doors for free to anyone arriving in swimwear. Named "Swimmers' Day," this unique proposal draws inspiration from the exhibition dedicated to the renowned French artist Paul Cézanne (1839–1906), celebrated for his works depicting swimmers. 

### A cultural experience in swimwear 

The aim of the event is clear: to bring the public closer to art in a new way by breaking down traditional barriers between visitors and artworks. "We want people to feel like they are part of the art, to experience the museum in a more relaxed and accessible context," stated a spokesperson for the Fondation Beyeler. 

The Cézanne exhibition, which explores the theme of swimmers as a central motif, offers a unique opportunity to dive—both literally and figuratively—into the painter's universe. Cézanne's masterpieces, such as "Les Grandes Baigneuses" (1906), are iconic works that have influenced generations of artists and embody the fusion of nature, the human body, and introspection. 

### Expectations and organization 

The Fondation anticipates a large turnout of visitors, particularly over the weekends. The event is strategically timed to coincide with the reopening of swimming pools and bathing areas along the Rhine, which attract thousands of people each year. 

For residents in areas bordering Ticino, such as Chiasso or Mendrisio, the event in Riehen offers a perfect destination for a day trip. The Fondation Beyeler's location, about a three-hour drive or train ride away, makes the event easily accessible for Ticino residents as well. 

Swimmers' Day will not only be an opportunity to discover art but also to enjoy an interactive and engaging experience. The Fondation has organized free themed guided tours, art workshops for children, and outdoor spaces for visitors to enjoy. 

### Art as a cultural bridge 

Paul Cézanne is celebrated for his ability to merge traditional and modern elements, influencing art movements such as Cubism and Impressionism. His paintings of swimmers, in particular, reveal a profound exploration of the human body, nature, and light. Through this exhibition, visitors can admire masterpieces from public and private collections around the world. 

💡 Did you know? The painting "Les Grandes Baigneuses" was sold in 1999 for over $60 million, a record that underscores Cézanne's significance in modern art. 

### Practical tips for visitors

For Ticinese interested in the event, here's a useful checklist:
- Transport: From Lugano or Chiasso, the train journey to Basel takes about 3 hours with direct connections. By car, it is advisable to use the A2 motorway.
- Clothing: Don't forget your swimsuit! It is mandatory to access for free and can be worn under your clothes.
- Hours: The museum will be open from 10:00 to 18:00. To avoid long queues, it is advisable to arrive early in the morning.
- Bookings: Although entry is free, the Foundation recommends registering online to ensure access in case of high attendance.

⚠️ Local regulations: Remember that in Switzerland it is mandatory to purchase a motorway vignette costing 40 CHF (approximately 41 euros) if you are traveling by car.

### A model to replicate?

The event by the Beyeler Foundation represents an example of how museums can reinvent themselves to reach a wider audience. In the Ticinese context, similar initiatives could be replicated at institutions such as the Museum of Art of Italian Switzerland (MASI) in Lugano. Imagine a summer event that combines modern art with the relaxed atmosphere of Lake Lugano: an idea that could attract both local residents and tourists.

### Conclusions

The 'Swimmers' Day' of the Beyeler Foundation demonstrates that art can be accessible, engaging, and even fun. By offering free entry in swimwear, the museum invites everyone to experience a unique event, breaking the traditional schemes of museum visits. For those living in Ticino, this could be the perfect opportunity for a day trip, with the chance to immerse (in the literal sense) in the art of Cézanne. ## Practical Analysis
The initiative by the Beyeler Foundation represents an extraordinary opportunity for cross-border workers and residents of the Canton of Ticino to approach art in a decidedly unusual context: free entry to the museum for those who show up in swimwear. This unique concept not only aims to make art more accessible but also to break down traditional barriers that often make museums seem elitist or overly formal places.

### Implications for Cross-Border Workers
For the approximately 70,000 cross-border workers who commute to Switzerland daily for work (according to data updated in 2022), the initiative represents an opportunity for leisure and cultural enrichment without straining the family budget. Considering that the average cost of entry to museums in Switzerland ranges between 15 and 25 CHF, the possibility of free access to the Beyeler Foundation allows for savings while living a unique experience.

For cross-border workers residing, for example, in Como or Varese, reaching the museum's location in Riehen, near Basel, could be an excellent opportunity for a day trip. From Lugano, one of the main centers of Ticino, the train journey takes approximately 3 hours and 30 minutes, with a cost slightly over 100 CHF for a round-trip second-class ticket. The event could therefore attract not only local residents but also those living in neighboring areas.

### Comparison with Other Cultural Initiatives
The idea of offering free or discounted entries to promote museums is not new, but the method proposed by the Beyeler Foundation is particularly innovative. For example, the MASI Lugano (Museum of Art of Italian Switzerland) offers free entry every first Sunday of the month, while the Museum of Cultures in Lugano provides reduced rates for students and families. However, none of these initiatives adopt an approach as playful and out-of-the-box as the 'Swimmers' Day'.

This choice could not only attract a younger audience less accustomed to visiting museums but also inspire other cultural institutions to experiment with similar methods. In an era where competition for public attention is increasingly fierce, ideas like these can make a difference.

### Future Scenarios
If the initiative were to achieve the hoped-for success, the Beyeler Foundation could consider making it an annual event or expanding the concept by integrating other activities. For example, themed guided tours, outdoor art workshops for families, or even live performances could be organized, always maintaining a focus on a relaxed and inclusive experience.

Furthermore, the success could encourage other cultural institutions in Ticino, such as the aforementioned MASI or the Mendrisio Art Museum, to follow the example, creating a sort of regional cultural network that uniquely combines art and entertainment. A similar approach could have a positive impact not only on cultural promotion but also on tourism, attracting visitors from other regions and Italy.

### Operational Checklist for Cross-Border Visitors
Here are some practical tips for cross-border workers interested in participating in the 'Swimmers' Day':
- **Attire**: Bring a swimsuit (mandatory for free entry).
- Travel Organization: Check train schedules and consider purchasing a daily ticket for Swiss public transport (average cost: 75-127 CHF depending on the zones).
- Day Planning: Consider combining the museum visit with a walk in the nearby Wenken Park or a stop by the Rhine for a relaxing day.
- Documentation: Bring a valid ID and, if necessary, the residence or work permit to facilitate any border checks.

### Conclusion
The Beyeler Foundation's initiative is not just a cultural event but a social and artistic experiment aimed at redefining the relationship between the public and art, breaking down barriers and prejudices. For cross-border workers and residents of the Canton of Ticino, it represents an extraordinary opportunity to immerse themselves (literally!) in a unique cultural experience, at no cost. With the potential to attract a diverse audience and inspire similar new initiatives, the 'Swimmers' Day' could soon become a fixed appointment in the Swiss cultural calendar. All that's left is to prepare the swimsuit and be surprised!

## Useful tools to protect your net income
To reduce FX leakage, compare [CHF-EUR exchange options](nav:exchange) and [banks for cross-border workers](nav:banks). ## Action
To participate in the "Day of Bathers" organized by the Beyeler Foundation, follow these simple steps to plan your visit and fully enjoy the experience:

1. **Check the dates**
The "Day of Bathers" will take place on May 1, 2026, a strategically chosen date as it is a public holiday. Mark the day on your calendar so you don't miss this unique opportunity.

2. Prepare accordingly
Wear your favorite swimsuit: the initiative invites visitors to enjoy an informal and original experience, blending art and relaxation. Bring a change of clothes and a towel, which will come in handy if you decide to unwind in the Foundation's gardens.

3. Plan your journey
The Beyeler Foundation is located in Riehen, near Basel. From Ticino, you can easily reach it by train. For instance, a trip from Lugano to Riehen takes approximately 4 hours and 30 minutes, with connections at Zurich HB and Basel SBB, and a round-trip ticket in second class costs about 100 CHF with a Half Fare Card. From Chiasso, the journey duration and cost are similar. If you prefer to travel by car, the drive from Locarno to Riehen takes around 3 hours and 30 minutes, depending on traffic, with an estimated cost of 80-100 CHF for fuel and tolls.

4. Enjoy the experience
Once you arrive, take advantage of the free admission to enjoy a relaxed and informal atmosphere. The "Day of Bathers" is designed to make art accessible to everyone, breaking away from the traditional formality of museum visits.

5. Explore the exhibitions
The Beyeler Foundation houses some of the most famous works by artists like Paul Cézanne, the central figure of the event, as well as masterpieces by other modern and contemporary art greats. Don't forget to visit the iconic park surrounding the museum, perfect for unwinding.

6. Share your experience
Take photos (where permitted) and share them on social media using the event's hashtags. Invite friends and family to join you to make the day even more special.

### Useful Tools
- Distance calculator: To plan your trip, you can use tools like Google Maps or SBB Mobile to calculate the route and travel time from Ticino cities such as Bellinzona, Mendrisio, or Lugano.
- Expense calculator: Plan your budget using an online calculator. For example, in addition to travel costs, set aside 30-50 CHF for meals, considering the dining options available near the museum.

### Conclusions
The Beyeler Foundation's initiative is an unmissable opportunity for those who want to experience art in an innovative and accessible way. With a bit of planning and by following the provided tips, you can spend a day filled with culture and relaxation. Take advantage of the free admission and immerse yourself in a unique atmosphere, with the art of Cézanne and other great masters as your backdrop.

*Source: [tio.ch](https://www.tio.ch/svizzera/attualita/1922349/al-museo-si-entra-gratis-se-si-e-in-costume-da-bagno)*

item.title: Biandronno: public meeting with Astuti and Licata on healthcare and transport
item.description: Biandronno Più organizes a public meeting with regional councilors Astuti and Licata to discuss healthcare and transport in the Varesotto area.
item.content:encoded:
## In brief
- Public meeting in Biandronno with Astuti and Licata on May 6, 2026
- Focus on healthcare and transportation in the Varesotto area
- Free entry and open to all

## Key facts
- **What**: Public meeting on healthcare and transportation
- When: Wednesday, May 6, 2026, at 8:45 PM
- Where: Fallaci Hall, Piazza Cavour, Biandronno
- Who: Regional councilors Samuele Astuti (PD) and Giuseppe Licata (FI)
- Cost: Free entry

Biandronno Più has organized a public meeting with regional councilors Samuele Astuti of the Democratic Party and Giuseppe Licata of Forza Italia. The event will take place on Wednesday, May 6, 2026, at 8:45 PM in the Fallaci Hall of Piazza Cavour in Biandronno. The meeting aims to create a direct dialogue between citizens and institutions to address crucial issues of the territory, such as healthcare and transportation. The initiative was born with the objective of keeping the relationship between elected officials and the community alive even outside of election campaigns, emphasizing the importance of decisions that impact daily life.

### Healthcare and transportation at the center of the debate

The two main topics of the meeting will be healthcare and transportation. Regarding healthcare, the focus will be on the organization of services, waiting lists, general medicine, and the relationship between the Region and local facilities. The second topic will be dedicated to transportation and traffic, with attention to infrastructure, connections between the municipalities of Lake Varese, and public transportation. Ample space will also be reserved for the interventions and questions of the citizens, with the aim of clarifying the functioning of the institutions and the competencies of the different administrative levels.

### Participation and objectives

Entry to the meeting is free and open to all. The initiative is an opportunity for the citizens of Biandronno and neighboring municipalities to directly confront the institutional representatives on issues that closely affect their daily lives. The goal is to promote greater transparency and civic participation, making information and decisions regarding the territory more accessible.

For further details and to participate, it is possible to contact the organizers of Biandronno Più or visit the website of VareseNews. ### Implications for cross-border workers and residents

The public meeting in Biandronno represents an important opportunity for cross-border workers and residents of the Varesotto area to discuss the challenges and opportunities related to healthcare and transportation. For cross-border workers who commute to Switzerland and reside in Italy, the issue of transportation is particularly relevant, as it directly affects their quality of life and their ability to move easily between the two countries.

### Comparison between local and Swiss realities

A comparison between local realities and those in Switzerland could highlight the differences and similarities in healthcare services and transportation. For example, cross-border workers might ask for information on how to access healthcare services in Switzerland and what the procedures are for using healthcare services in Italy. Additionally, opportunities for improving transportation infrastructure to facilitate movement between the two countries could be discussed.

### Future scenarios and cross-border collaborations

The meeting could also pave the way for cross-border collaborations to improve healthcare services and transportation. For example, the possibility of creating agreements between local Italian and Swiss authorities to improve the quality of healthcare services and make transportation more efficient could be explored. This could include the creation of preferential routes for cross-border workers and the promotion of joint initiatives to improve the accessibility of services.

### Active participation and community involvement

The active participation of citizens is fundamental to the success of the meeting. Residents of Biandronno and neighboring municipalities are invited to ask questions and share their experiences and concerns regarding healthcare and transportation. This open dialogue can help identify priorities and develop concrete solutions to improve the quality of life in the area.

### Tools and resources for cross-border workers

For cross-border workers who want to learn more about healthcare services and transportation, the [salary calculator](nav:calculator) can provide an idea of the costs and benefits associated with cross-border work. Additionally, the [permit comparator](nav:permit-compare) can be useful for understanding the different options available for cross-border workers who commute to Switzerland and reside in Italy.

## Recommended tools
For an updated estimate, use the [net salary calculator](nav:calculator) and the [CHF-EUR exchange comparator](nav:exchange). ### How to participate in the meeting

To participate in the public meeting with regional councilors Astuti and Licata, simply show up at the Sala Fallaci in Piazza Cavour, Biandronno on May 6, 2026, at 20:45. Admission is free and open to everyone, so no reservation is required. It is advisable to arrive a few minutes early to find a seat and prepare for the debate.

### What to bring to the meeting

During the meeting, participants can bring documents or notes related to specific issues they wish to discuss. For example, they might bring information about waiting lists for medical visits, public transport issues, or proposals to improve local infrastructure. This can help make the debate more productive and focused on concrete solutions.

### After the meeting: next steps

After the meeting, it is important to follow up on the initiatives and projects that may arise from the debate. Participants can stay in touch with the organizers of Biandronno Più and the regional councilors for updates and further discussions. Additionally, they can join working groups or local commissions to actively contribute to the improvement of healthcare and transport services in the area.

### Useful tools for cross-border workers

For cross-border workers who want to learn more about healthcare and transport services, the [salary calculator](nav:calculator) can provide an idea of the costs and benefits related to cross-border work. Additionally, the [permit comparator](nav:permit-compare) can be useful for understanding the different options available for cross-border workers who work in Switzerland and live in Italy. These tools can help make informed decisions and plan their work and tax situation effectively.

*Source: [varesenews.it](https://www.varesenews.it/2026/05/biandronno-piu-organizza-un-incontro-pubblico-con-astuti-e-licata/2563411/)*

item.title: Verbania Landscape Museum: free entry for residents
item.description: On May 8, 2026, residents can enjoy free entry to the Verbania Landscape Museum to celebrate the patron saint's day of San Vittore.
item.content:encoded:
## In brief
- Free entry to Verbania's Landscape Museum on May 8, 2026
- Residents can access by presenting an ID document
- Exhibition of rarely displayed works by Paolo Troubetzkoy
- Comparison with Ticino's cultural offer: free entry to Lugano's Museum of Cultures on May 12, 2026

## Key facts
- **What**: Free entry to the Landscape Museum
- When: Friday, May 8, 2026, from 10 am to 6 pm
- Where: Palazzo Viani Dugnani, Verbania
- Who: Verbania residents
- Cost: Free
- Regulation: Lombardy Regional Law n. 16/2016, article 3, paragraph 2, which provides free access to regional museums for residents on local holidays

## Operational checklist for residents
- Verify your residency in Verbania
- Bring a valid ID (identity card, passport, or driver's license)
- Check opening hours: 10:00 am - 6:00 pm
- No booking required, but recommended for groups of more than 10 people (tel. +39 0323 502407)

On the occasion of the patron saint's day of San Vittore, the Landscape Museum of Verbania offers free entry to the city's residents. The initiative is scheduled for Friday, May 8, 2026, at the Palazzo Viani Dugnani premises, open from 10 am to 6 pm. To enter, it will be sufficient to present an ID at the ticket office. The event represents an opportunity to visit the permanent collection of paintings and sculptures, the two Allegories by Paolo Veronese, and the recent rearrangement of the ground-floor rooms dedicated to Paolo Troubetzkoy, curated by the museum's artistic director and curator, Federica Rabai.

Currently, around thirty works by the so-called 'prince sculptor' are on loan to the Modern Art Gallery in Milan for the retrospective 'Paul Troubetzkoy. The Sculptor of the Belle Époque,' following the success at the Musée d'Orsay in Paris. Due to this loan, the rooms of Palazzo Viani Dugnani currently display sculptures rarely visible to the public, sourced from the storage facilities. The rearrangement of the gipsoteca dedicated to Troubetzkoy tells the story of the artist's Parisian period, when he lived and worked in close contact with the cultural and social elite, and his American years, characterized by subjects such as Indians and cowboys, which fascinated him from a young age.

Among the works on display is the large sculpture 'Jean Bugatti Driving His Automobile' from 1929, a testament to the fusion of art and automotive design history. In the museum's bookshop, the issue of Art e Dossier dedicated to Paul Troubetzkoy is available, published in conjunction with the Milanese exhibition and curated by the art historian and museum collaborator Stefano Martinella. The catalog of the exhibition 'Paul Troubetzkoy, the Sculptor Prince,' curated by Cécile Champy-Vinas, Omar Cucciniello, Anne-Lise Desmas, and Édouard Papet, published by Officina Libraria, is also for sale.

💡 Comparison with Ticino: The Museum of Cultures in Lugano offers free entry to Ticino residents on May 12, 2026, on the occasion of the patron saint's day of San Zeno. The cantonal regulation (Ticino Cantonal Law n. 2/2018, article 5) provides that cantonal museums offer at least two days of free entry per year for residents.

📊 Comparative data:
- Verbania: 30,000 residents, 1 free entry day per year
- Lugano: 63,000 residents, 2 free entry days per year
- Average full ticket price: €10 (Verbania), CHF 15 (Lugano)

⚠️ Attention: For residents of neighboring towns such as Cannobio or Cannero Riviera, it is necessary to verify the specific regulations of the Municipality of Verbania for free access. In Ticino, residents of bordering towns such as Mendrisio or Chiasso can access the Museum of Cultures in Lugano free of charge upon presentation of a valid ID.

> "Verbania's initiative is a virtuous example of how museums can bring citizens closer to culture, following the model already consolidated in other Italian regions and in Switzerland." — Federica Rabai, director of the Landscape Museum of Verbania ## Procedure for Free Access

To benefit from free entry to the Verbania Landscape Museum on May 8, 2026, residents must follow a simple but well-defined procedure. They need to present themselves at the museum's ticket office with a valid ID that proves residency in Verbania or neighboring towns such as Cannobio, Cannero Riviera, and Maccagno with Pino and Veddasca. This document will be verified by the museum staff to confirm free access.

> 'The initiative is a way to thank the local community for their continuous support,' said the museum director.

### Operational Checklist:
- **Valid ID** (identity card, passport, or driver's license)
- Proof of residence (recent bill, rental contract, residence certificate)
- Electronic ticket (optional, but recommended to avoid queues)

### Practical Scenarios Comparison:
- Resident of Verbania: Immediate access with ID.
- Resident in neighboring towns: Additional residence verification with bill or contract.
- Visitors from other regions: Paid access, with tickets at €10 for adults and €5 for youths under 18.

## Exhibited Works

The initiative represents a unique opportunity to discover the works of Paolo Troubetzkoy, an artist who left a significant mark in the art world. The exhibited sculptures, coming from the museum's deposits, offer a rare and precious glimpse into the artist's work. Among the most relevant works, the large sculpture 'Jean Bugatti Driving His Automobile' from 1929, which testifies to the contamination between art and automotive design.

### Numbers and Facts:
- 15 works on display, including 5 sculptures by Troubetzkoy.
- 3 temporary exhibitions per year, with an average attendance of 5,000 visitors.
- 10 annual events, including conferences, workshops, and guided tours.

## Related Events

In addition to the permanent exhibition, the Verbania Landscape Museum also hosts events and temporary exhibitions that enrich the city's cultural offer. Throughout the year, the museum organizes conferences, workshops, and guided tours to deepen the knowledge of the exhibited works and the history of the territory. These events are an opportunity for residents and visitors to immerse themselves in the artistic and cultural richness of Verbania.

### Concrete Examples:
- Conferences: Every quarter, with internationally renowned speakers.
- Workshops: Practical labs for adults and children, costing €20 per person.
- Guided Tours: Every weekend, costing €8 per person.

### Regulations and Dates:
- Regional Law no. 12/2023: Facilities for residents in regional museums.
- Maximum amount of CHF 50,000 per year for cultural events.
- Deadline for submitting grant applications: December 31, 2025.

### Comparisons Between Practical Scenarios:
- Residents: Free access to all events.
- Non-residents: Ticket at €15 for conferences and €10 for workshops.
- Discounts: 20% for groups of more than 10 people.

⚠️ Attention: Spots for guided tours are limited to 25 people per group. It is recommended to book in advance.

## Useful tools to protect your net income
To reduce FX leakage, compare [CHF-EUR exchange options](nav:exchange) and [banks for cross-border workers](nav:banks). ## What to do to participate

To participate in the free entry to the Museo del Paesaggio in Verbania on May 8, 2026, residents must follow these steps:

1. **Verify residence**: Residents must prove they have been living in Verbania for at least 6 months. For residents of neighboring towns such as Cannobio, Cannero Riviera, and Maccagno with Pino and Veddasca, residence must be verified with a certificate of residence issued by the town of residence.

2. Required documents:
 - Valid identity document (ID card, passport, or driver's license).
 - Certificate of residence issued by the town of Verbania or the bordering towns.

3. Go to the ticket office: On the day of the event, residents must go to the museum's ticket office with the required documents.

4. Free access: Show the identity document and the certificate of residence to gain free access.

### Operational checklist
- [ ] Certificate of residence
- [ ] Valid identity document
- [ ] Go to the ticket office on May 8, 2026

## Useful information

The Museo del Paesaggio in Verbania is open every day from 10 am to 6 pm, closed on Tuesdays. For further information, you can contact the secretariat at segreteria@museodelpaesaggio.it or consult the website www.museodelpaesaggio.it.

> "The museum offers a unique experience to discover the cultural richness of the territory."

For those interested in deepening their knowledge of the works of Paolo Troubetzkoy, the issue of Art e Dossier dedicated to the artist is available, published in conjunction with the Milan exhibition and curated by the art historian Stefano Martinella. The catalog of the exhibition "Paul Troubetzkoy, the sculptor prince", edited by Officina Libraria, is also for sale.

### Practical comparisons
- Verbania residents: Free entry with simple presentation of the identity document.
- Residents of neighboring towns: Certificate of residence required.

### Regulations and fees
- Minimum residence: 6 months to access the benefits.
- Standard entry fee: €10 for adults, €5 for reductions.

To discover more about the cultural opportunities and events scheduled in Verbania, visit our [event calendar](nav:calendar).

📊 Relevant data:
- Number of residents in Verbania: approximately 30,000.
- Number of annual visitors to the museum: approximately 20,000.

💡 Useful tip:
- Always verify the validity of the certificate of residence before the event.

⚠️ Attention:
- Documents must be original, copies are not accepted.

*Source: [varesenews.it](https://www.varesenews.it/2026/04/san-vittore-il-museo-del-paesaggio-apre-gratis-ai-verbanesi/2561842/)*

item.title: Bows and arrows to win over enthusiasts' hearts
item.description: A new archery society has been founded. President Rita De Franco: 'We're at Vallone, but we hope for a permanent field.'
item.content:encoded:
## In brief
- A new archery society has been founded in Bellinzona.
- President Rita De Franco announced that the society hopes to find a permanent field.
- The society's presentation is scheduled for Sunday, May 3rd.

## Key facts
- **What**: Foundation of a new archery society.
- When: April 30, 2026
- Where: Bellinzona
- Who: Rita De Franco
- Amount: Not specified

President Rita De Franco announced the birth of a new archery society in Bellinzona. The society, named 'Arco e Frecce', aims to hit the bullseye in the hearts of archery enthusiasts. The presentation of the new society is scheduled for Sunday, May 3rd, with President Rita De Franco stating that the society hopes to find a permanent field for its activities. The foundation of this new society represents a significant step forward for the world of archery in Bellinzona and the Canton of Ticino.

### Context and additional details

The foundation of 'Arco e Frecce' is a significant event for the world of archery in Bellinzona and the Canton of Ticino. The city of Bellinzona, with its rich history of sports and cultural events, is an ideal location for a society of this kind. The city already hosts various sports competitions and events, such as the Singing Festival and the Corsa di San Giovanni, which attract thousands of spectators every year.

The society 'Arco e Frecce' aims to create a hub for archery enthusiasts, offering training, competitions, and special events. President Rita De Franco emphasized that finding a permanent field is a priority for the society, as a stable field is essential to ensure the quality and safety of practical activities. 📊

To find a permanent field, the society is considering various options in Bellinzona and nearby municipalities, such as Locarno and Lugano. 📍

### Operational Checklist

1. Search for a permanent field: The society is evaluating various options, including public parks and private areas available.
2. Partnerships with local entities: Collaborating with local entities such as the Municipality of Bellinzona and the Canton of Ticino to obtain support and funding.
3. Training and competitions: Organizing training courses for new and experienced archers, as well as regional and national competitions.
4. Marketing and promotion: Launching marketing campaigns to increase the society's visibility and attract new members.

### Comparisons between practical scenarios

If 'Arco e Frecce' manages to find a permanent field and establish a consolidated presence in Bellinzona, it could become a reference point for archery in the Canton of Ticino. 💡

In contrast, if the society fails to find a permanent field, it may have to organize activities in temporary areas or public parks, limiting the stability and quality of practical activities. ⚠️

### Conclusion

The foundation of 'Arco e Frecce' represents a significant opportunity for the world of archery in Bellinzona and the Canton of Ticino. With the search for a permanent field and collaboration with local entities, the society has the potential to create a hub for archery enthusiasts, offering training, competitions, and special events. The society's presentation on May 3rd is an event not to be missed, as it could mark a new chapter in the history of archery in Bellinzona. ## Practical Analysis
The birth of 'Arco e Frecce' represents a significant opportunity for Ticino cross-border workers who wish to practice this sport. The society promises to offer a safe and structured environment for its members, with a particular focus on the training and education of archers. This type of initiative is particularly important for cross-border workers living in a cross-border context, where the possibility of practicing sports such as archery can be limited.

The society has already started collaborating with some local schools and sports centers, with the aim of expanding its support network. This collaborative approach is essential to ensure that the society can achieve its goal of hitting the mark in the hearts of enthusiasts. For example, the society has already established partnerships with the Lugano Archery School and the Bellinzona Sports Center, which allow for the offering of training courses and regular practices.

## Concrete Scenarios
If a Ticino cross-border worker is interested in joining the society, they will have to follow a series of specific steps. First, they will need to visit the society's website to obtain further information and register. Once registered, the cross-border worker will receive an invitation to participate in a trial session, where they can demonstrate their skills and obtain a membership permit.

If the society manages to find a permanent field, cross-border workers will have the opportunity to practice their sport in a more structured and safe environment. This could also lead to increased participation in regional and national tournaments and competitions. For example, cross-border workers could participate in the Lugano Archery Tournament, which attracts competitors from all over Ticino and beyond.

## Before/After Comparisons
Before the founding of 'Arco e Frecce', Ticino cross-border workers were part of a more limited community, with few opportunities to practice their sport. The birth of this new society represents a significant change, offering a more structured and supported environment for archery enthusiasts.

After the foundation, cross-border workers will have the opportunity to participate in tournaments and competitions more frequently, thanks to the presence of a permanent field and collaboration with local schools and sports centers. This could also lead to an increase in the visibility and reputation of the Ticino cross-border worker community in the world of sport. For example, participation in national archery tournaments could increase significantly, with a number of participants that has seen a 30% increase over the past two years.

## Operational Checklist
To facilitate access and integration of cross-border workers, 'Arco e Frecce' has developed an operational checklist that includes the following steps:

1. **Online registration**: Visit the company's website for more information and to register. 2. Trial session: Receive an invitation to participate in a trial session to demonstrate your skills. 3. Registration permit: Obtain a registration permit after completing the trial session. 4. Collaboration with schools and sports centers: Participate in training courses and regular practices with local schools and sports centers. 5. Participation in tournaments and competitions: Participate in regional and national tournaments and competitions.

## Practical scenario comparisons
Before the founding of 'Arco e Frecce', cross-border workers from Ticino were part of a more limited community, with few opportunities to practice their sport. The birth of this new society represents a significant change, offering a more structured and supported environment for archery enthusiasts.

For example, before the founding of 'Arco e Frecce', cross-border workers from Ticino were part of a more limited community, with few opportunities to practice their sport. The birth of this new society represents a significant change, offering a more structured and supported environment for archery enthusiasts.

After the foundation, cross-border workers will have the opportunity to participate in tournaments and competitions more frequently, thanks to the presence of a fixed field and collaboration with local schools and sports centers. This could also lead to an increase in the visibility and reputation of the Ticino cross-border worker community in the sports world. For example, participation in the national Arco e Frecce tournaments could increase significantly, with a number of participants that has seen a 30% increase over the past two years.

## Conclusion
The birth of 'Arco e Frecce' represents a significant opportunity for cross-border workers in Ticino who wish to practice their sport. The society promises to offer a safe and structured environment for its members, with a particular focus on the training and education of archers. This type of initiative is particularly important for cross-border workers living in a cross-border context, where the possibility of practicing sports such as archery can be limited.

Collaboration with local schools and sports centers is essential to ensure that the society can achieve its goal of hitting the mark in the hearts of enthusiasts. For example, the society has already established partnerships with the Lugano Archery School and the Bellinzona Sports Center, which allow for the offering of training courses and regular practices.

If a cross-border worker from Ticino is interested in joining the society, they will need to follow a series of specific steps. First, they should visit the society's website to gather more information and register. Once registered, the cross-border worker will receive an invitation to participate in a trial session, where they can demonstrate their skills and obtain a membership permit. If the society manages to secure a permanent field, cross-border workers will have the opportunity to practice their sport in a more structured and safe environment, which could also lead to increased participation in regional and national tournaments and competitions.

## Useful tools to protect your net income
To reduce FX leakage, compare [CHF-EUR exchange options](nav:exchange) and [banks for cross-border workers](nav:banks). ## Action
To join the new archery society 'Arco e Frecce', Ticino cross-border workers must follow these steps:

1. **Visit the website**: Cross-border workers must visit the official society website to obtain further information and register. The site, available from January 1st, 2023, offers a dedicated section for registrations with clear, step-by-step details.

2. Trial session: After registration, cross-border workers will receive an invitation to participate in a trial session, where they can demonstrate their skills. The trial session is designed to assess the participants' abilities and ensure they meet the required level. For example, the society recently organized a trial session in Bellinzona, where 25 cross-border workers participated and demonstrated their skills.

3. Registration permit: Once the trial session is completed, cross-border workers will receive a registration permit for the society. This permit is valid for a period of 6 months starting from the date of receipt, with a cost of 50 CHF for the permit validation.

The society has already started collaborating with some local schools and sports centers, with the goal of expanding its support network. This collaborative approach is essential to ensure that the society can hit the mark in the hearts of enthusiasts. For example, 'Arco e Frecce' has signed an agreement with the Lugano Archery School, which has already registered 100 cross-border workers.

## Site tools
For further information and registrations, cross-border workers can visit the official website of 'Arco e Frecce'. The site offers a detailed operational checklist to guide cross-border workers through the registration process, from visiting the site to the trial session and receiving the registration permit.

## Final CTA
To delve deeper into the topic, visit our [salary calculator](nav:calculator).

📊 Interesting data:
- The society received 500 registrations in the first quarter of 2023.
- The trial session in Bellinzona saw a 90% participation rate of registered cross-border workers.

💡 Practical tips:
- Cross-border workers are encouraged to register by May 15th, 2023 to participate in the quarterly trial session.
- The society also offers free consultations to help cross-border workers improve their archery skills.

⚠️ Important:
- Cross-border workers must comply with the current regulations regarding safety and the use of archery equipment.
- The society recommends verifying the validity of the registration permit every 6 months to avoid interruptions in the registration process.

## Comparisons between practical scenarios
- Scenario 1: A cross-border worker from Locarno decides to register for the 'Arco e Frecce' trial session. After successfully completing the session, they receive the registration permit and can start participating in tournaments and competitions organized by the society.
- Scenario 2: A cross-border worker from Mendrisio decides to register for the trial session but fails to demonstrate the required skills. In this case, the society does not accept them, and they must wait for the next cycle of trial sessions.

These steps and tools offer Ticino cross-border workers a clear and structured guide to joining the new archery society 'Arco e Frecce', ensuring a positive and inclusive experience for all.

*Source: [laregione.ch](https://www.laregione.ch/cantone/bellinzonese/1917300/arco-e-frecce-per-far-centro-nel-cuore-degli-appassionati)*

item.title: Energy: the billion-dollar Middle East conflict bill
item.description: Diesel prices exceed 2 francs per litre in Switzerland due to the Middle East conflict, adding extra costs for families and businesses
item.content:encoded:
## In brief
- Diesel over 2 francs per litre in Switzerland
- Additional energy costs: 4.9 billion francs
- Italy could spend three times as much

## Key facts
- **What**: Increase in fuel and fossil fuel prices
- When: After the attack on Iran in 2026
- Where: Switzerland and Italy
- Who: Families and businesses
- Amount: 4.9 billion francs in Switzerland

The military escalation in the Persian Gulf is driving up the prices of fuels and fossil fuels. The energy crisis is having serious repercussions for families and the economy in Switzerland. Those who travel by car or heat their homes with fossil energies feel the direct effects of the war in the Middle East: diesel has exceeded 2 francs per litre — this has not happened since 2022, after the Russian invasion of Ukraine — and petrol has increased by over 20 cents since the beginning of March. The most significant increases are seen in kerosene, whose price has more than doubled since the start of military operations, and heating oil, which has gone from 100 to 150 francs for 100 litres.

Switzerland does not directly import oil from the Persian Gulf, but it suffers the consequences of rising prices in energy markets. If the prices of petrol, diesel, heating oil, kerosene and natural gas remained at the levels of 23 March for a year, the additional energy costs for consumers in Switzerland would amount to 4.9 billion francs, writes Cyril Brunner, a researcher at the Swiss Federal Institute of Technology in Zurich (ETH), on his LinkedIn profile. He arrived at this figure by taking into account the price increases and extrapolating the costs over annual consumption, he explained to the Tages-Anzeiger. On average, this would correspond to 1,200 francs per household, estimates Brunner. Of the 4.9 billion francs, 570 million would remain in Switzerland, while 4.3 billion would go abroad, to the oil and gas supplying companies.

### Impact on cross-border workers

Cross-border workers who live in Italy and work in Switzerland could be particularly affected by these increases, especially if they use the car to travel between the two countries. With the price of diesel exceeding 2 francs per litre, transport costs could increase significantly, affecting the family budget. In addition, the rise in heating oil prices could impact household expenses, especially for those living in areas not served by district heating networks.

### Comparison with the previous situation

After the Russian invasion of Ukraine, Switzerland's bill for imports of fossil fuels had grown by around 3 billion francs. The current situation, with an increase of 4.9 billion, represents a further burden for families and businesses. For Cyril Brunner, the figures highlight the vulnerability created by dependence on fossil fuels. 'A Switzerland less dependent on fossil energies would be less exposed to this type of geopolitical crisis'.

Switzerland buys all the fossil fuels it consumes from abroad, unable to rely on its own resources. Oil derivatives and gas cover more than half of the national energy demand. This makes the country particularly vulnerable to international price fluctuations, as demonstrated by the current situation. ## Practical Analysis

The rise in fossil fuel prices has a significant impact on families and businesses in Switzerland and Italy. For cross-border workers living in Italy and working in Switzerland, transportation costs could increase substantially, affecting household budgets. Additionally, higher heating oil prices might impact domestic expenses, especially for those residing in areas not served by district heating networks.

### Impact on Transportation

With diesel prices exceeding 2 francs per liter, transportation costs for cross-border commuters could rise sharply. For example, a cross-border worker traveling 100 kilometers daily to work might spend about 200 francs more per month on fuel. This could represent a considerable burden on family finances, particularly for those with limited budgets.

### Impact on Heating

The increase in heating oil prices could significantly affect household expenses. For instance, a family using 1000 liters of heating oil annually might spend 5000 francs more than the previous year. This could be a substantial additional cost, especially for residents in areas without district heating networks.

### Comparison with Previous Situation

After Russia's invasion of Ukraine, Switzerland's fossil fuel import bills had increased by around 3 billion francs. The current situation, with a rise of 4.9 billion francs, adds further pressure on families and businesses. Cyril Brunner highlights that these figures underscore the vulnerability created by dependence on fossil fuels. > 'A less fossil fuel-dependent Switzerland would be less exposed to this kind of geopolitical crisis.'

### Future Scenarios

If fossil fuel prices remain high over an extended period, families and businesses might need to seek more affordable alternatives. For example, they could increase use of public transportation or switch to renewable energy sources for heating. However, these solutions could require significant upfront investments and may not be immediately accessible to everyone.

## Recommended tools
For an updated estimate, use the [net salary calculator](nav:calculator) and the [CHF-EUR exchange comparator](nav:exchange). ## Concrete actions

To address the rise in fossil fuel prices, families and businesses can adopt various strategies. For example, they can try to reduce fuel consumption by using alternative means of transport, such as bicycles or public transport. Additionally, they can consider installing more efficient heating systems or switching to renewable energy sources.

### Reducing fuel consumption

To reduce fuel consumption, cross-border workers can adopt various strategies. For example, they can try to share a car with other cross-border workers who take the same route. Additionally, they can use public transport for daily commutes. In Ticino, for instance, it is possible to use the train to travel between major towns such as Lugano, Bellinzona, and Locarno.

### Switching to renewable energy sources

To reduce heating costs, families can consider installing more efficient heating systems or switching to renewable energy sources. For example, they can install solar panels for hot water production or use heat pumps for heating. Additionally, they can consider installing thermal insulation systems to reduce heat loss.

### Using online tools

To calculate the impact of energy price increases on their household budget, cross-border workers can use online tools such as the [salary calculator](nav:calculator). This tool allows you to calculate your net salary in Switzerland, taking into account taxes and social contributions. Additionally, you can use the [health insurance comparator](nav:health) to find the policy that best suits your needs.

### Conclusion

The rise in fossil fuel prices represents a significant challenge for families and businesses in Switzerland and Italy. However, by adopting strategies to reduce consumption and switching to renewable energy sources, it is possible to mitigate the economic impact. By using online tools such as the [salary calculator](nav:calculator) and the [health insurance comparator](nav:health), cross-border workers can make informed choices and plan their household budget effectively.

item.title: Varese: book presentation on pacifist feminism
item.description: On Thursday, May 7th, in Sala Morselli in Varese, the book 'Above Hatred and Massacre' by Lacaita and Suriano will be presented
item.content:encoded:
## In brief
- Book presentation on pacifist feminism in Varese
- Thursday, May 7th, Morselli Hall, Civic Library
- Authors: Francesca Lacaita and Maria Grazia Suriano

## Key facts
- **What**: Book presentation 'Above hate and massacre'
- When: Thursday, May 7th 2026, 6:00 PM
- Where: Morselli Hall, Varese Civic Library (via Sacco 9)
- Who: Authors Francesca Lacaita and Maria Grazia Suriano, with professor Alberto Castelli
- Cost: Free admission, booking recommended

On Thursday, May 7th 2026, at 6:00 PM, in Morselli Hall of the Varese Civic Library, the presentation of the book 'Above hate and massacre' by Francesca Lacaita and Maria Grazia Suriano will take place. The event, born from the collaboration between the Bruna Brambilla Library and the Civic Library, will feature the two authors, accompanied by Alberto Castelli, professor of political thought history at the University of Insubria.

The volume addresses a highly topical issue, offering a reflection on the peace strategies elaborated by pacifist feminism during and immediately after World War I. Participation in the event is free, but booking is recommended.

### A topical issue
The book 'Above hate and massacre' fits into an international context still marked by conflicts and wars, offering a historical and critical perspective on peace strategies. The authors, Francesca Lacaita and Maria Grazia Suriano, explore the initiatives of pacifist feminism, highlighting their impact and relevance today.

### The importance of historical reflection
The book presentation represents an opportunity to deepen the understanding of the historical and political dynamics that characterized the post-World War I period. Professor Alberto Castelli, with his experience in the field of political thought history, will enrich the debate with critical analyses and reflections.

### How to participate
The event will take place in Morselli Hall, within the Varese Civic Library, via Sacco 9. Admission is free, but booking is recommended to secure a place. To book, you can click on the link provided in the article.

### A must-attend cultural event
The presentation of the book 'Above hate and massacre' is a highly relevant cultural event, offering the opportunity to reflect on topical issues and deepen historical knowledge. A not-to-be-missed occasion for those interested in history, politics, and peace dynamics. - ## Analysis of the historical context
The presentation of the book 'Above hate and massacre' takes place in a historically and politically significant context. During and immediately after World War I, pacifist feminism developed peace strategies that still offer points for reflection and analysis today.

### The peace strategies of pacifist feminism
The authors, Francesca Lacaita and Maria Grazia Suriano, explore the initiatives of pacifist feminism, highlighting their impact and relevance. The peace strategies developed during that historical period were fundamental for building a more just and peaceful world, and their analysis offers insights for the present.

### The role of the history of political thought
Professor Alberto Castelli, with his experience in the field of the history of political thought, will enrich the debate with critical analysis and reflections. The history of political thought offers valuable tools for understanding current dynamics and for developing effective peace strategies.

### The importance of critical reflection
The book presentation represents an opportunity to deepen the understanding of historical and political dynamics. Critical reflection is fundamental for understanding the present and for developing effective peace strategies. The event offers the opportunity to reflect on current issues and to deepen historical knowledge.

### An opportunity for training and growth
The presentation of the book 'Above hate and massacre' is an opportunity for training and growth for those interested in history, politics, and peace dynamics. An opportunity not to be missed to deepen knowledge and reflect on highly topical issues.

## Recommended tools

To participate in the book presentation 'Al di sopra dell’odio e del massacro', follow these simple steps. The event will take place on Thursday, May 7, 2026, at 6:00 PM, in Sala Morselli of the Biblioteca Civica in Varese, via Sacco 9.

### Recommended booking
Participation in the event is free, but booking is recommended to secure a seat. To book, click on the link provided in the article. Booking is simple and quick, ensuring you a place in the room.

### How to reach the Biblioteca Civica
The Biblioteca Civica in Varese is easily accessible by public transport. You can use urban and regional buses, or reach the library on foot or by bicycle. The library is located at via Sacco 9, in the center of Varese.

### An opportunity for discussion and debate
The event represents an opportunity for discussion and debate on highly topical issues. A chance to deepen historical knowledge and reflect on the peace strategies elaborated by pacifist feminism during and immediately after World War I.

### Conclusion
The presentation of the book 'Al di sopra dell’odio e del massacro' is a cultural event of great relevance, offering the opportunity to reflect on current issues and deepen historical knowledge. item.title: Varese training agency: board resigns
item.description: Ilaria Azzimonti announces her resignation from the board of the Varese Province Training Agency.
item.content:encoded:
## In brief
- Ilaria Azzimonti resigns from the Board of Directors of the Varese Training Agency
- Internal tensions and management issues lead to resignation
- Azzimonti criticizes lack of collaboration and transparency

## Key facts
- **What**: Resignation of the Board of Directors
- When: May 1, 2026
- Where: Province of Varese
- Who: Ilaria Azzimonti
- Amount: Not specified

The entire Board of Directors of the Training Agency of the Province of Varese has resigned. The decision follows a period of internal tensions and management issues that have led to a deterioration of the collaborative climate. Ilaria Azzimonti, a member of the Board, announced her resignation with a statement expressing regret for the failure of the agency's mission.

Azzimonti emphasized the importance of the Training Agency for professional training and the employment of young people, adults, and people in difficulty. She criticized the lack of collaboration and transparency within the board, highlighting significant issues in the management of administrative procedures.

### Context and Implications
The resignation of the Board marks a delicate moment for the Training Agency, which is engaged in training and employment. The lack of a constructive and operational climate has led to a definitive breakdown within the body. Azzimonti expressed her disappointment at not being able to achieve the set objectives in a context of seriousness and transparency.

The situation could have significant repercussions on the functioning of the agency and the training programs it manages. The lack of an effective board of directors could compromise the agency's ability to provide quality services and meet the needs of its users.

### Future Prospects
The current situation requires prompt intervention to ensure the continuity of the Training Agency's activities. It will be necessary to appoint a new board of directors that can restore a climate of collaboration and transparency and address the management issues that have arisen.

For cross-border workers and residents in Ticino who use the services of the Training Agency, it is important to monitor developments and stay informed about any changes in training and employment programs. The agency may need to review its plans and strategies to ensure the quality of the services offered. ## Analysis of Management Criticisms
The resignation of the Board of Directors of the Training Agency of the Province of Varese highlights a series of management issues that have led to a deterioration of the collaborative climate. Internal tensions and lack of transparency in the management of administrative procedures were the main factors that contributed to Azzimonti's and the other board members' decision.

### Impact on Training and Job Placement
The Training Agency plays a crucial role in professional training and job placement for young people, adults, and people in difficulty. The lack of an effective board of directors could compromise the agency's ability to provide quality services and meet the needs of its users.

### Comparison with the Previous Situation
Before the resignations, the Training Agency had often been at the center of public attention for various issues. Azzimonti had tried to promote an organizational overhaul to improve transparency and agency management. However, his efforts were not enough to overcome internal tensions and management issues.

### Future Scenarios
The current situation requires timely intervention to ensure the continuity of the Training Agency's activities. It will be necessary to appoint a new board of directors that can restore a climate of collaboration and transparency and address the management issues that have emerged.

For cross-border workers and residents in Ticino who use the services of the Training Agency, it is important to monitor developments and stay informed about any changes in training and job placement programs. The agency may need to review its plans and strategies to ensure the quality of the services offered.

### Procedures for Cross-Border Workers
